project.goal: Teach beginners how to use Git branching in under 90 seconds
project.audience: Software engineering students with no prior Git experience

transcript_segments.text: Hey everyone, welcome back. Today we are going to talk about Git branching.
Branching lets you work on new features without touching the main codebase. Think of it like a parallel universe for your code.
To create a branch, just type git checkout dash b and then your branch name. It is that simple.
Now you can make changes, commit them, and your main branch stays completely untouched. When you are ready, you merge back in.
Here is a quick demo. I will create a branch called feature-login, add a file, commit it, and then switch back to main.
See? Main is clean. The new file only exists on the feature branch. This is the core workflow used by every professional team.
That is basically it. Try creating your first branch today and let me know in the comments how it goes. See you next time.
